title: Identifying Oriented Spin Space Groups and Related Physical Properties Using an Online Platform FINDSPINGROUP
authors: Yutong Yu, Xiaobing Chen, Yanzhou Zhu, Yuhui Li, Renzheng Xiong, Jiayu Li, Yuntian Liu, Qihang Liu
abstract: Unconventional magnets that combine antiferromagnetic structures with ferromagnetic-like responses are essential for the development of next-generation spintronics. Their emergent properties are fundamentally dictated by the interplay between exchange-driven magnetic geometry and spin-orbit coupling, which are described by spin space group (SSG) and magnetic space group (MSG) frameworks, respectively. However, the lack of direct correspondence between these frameworks, developed in different eras, hinders the systematic tracking of symmetry evolution of these intertwined physical contributions. In this work, we introduce FINDSPINGROUP, a computational architecture that implements the recently emerged, oriented spin space group framework to unify SSG and MSG descriptions. By automating the tracking of symmetry-breaking pathways from the non-relativistic to the relativistic limit, this online platform enables the classification of magnetic phases and the derivation of symmetry-constrained tensors for phenomena such as momentum-dependent spin splitting and the anomalous Hall effect. By standardizing data exchange through the spin crystallographic information file, this architecture establishes a computational infrastructure for the high-throughput discovery and design of unconventional magnets.
summary: This paper presents FINDSPINGROUP, a new online platform designed to bridge the gap between spin space group and magnetic space group frameworks. By using the recently developed Oriented Spin Space Group (OSSG) framework, the tool allows researchers to track how symmetry-breaking pathways lead to emergent phenomena like the Anomalous Hall Effect and spin splitting. It introduces a standardized '.scif' file format for sharing magnetic symmetry data. The platform is particularly useful for the high-throughput discovery of unconventional magnets, such as altermagnets, by automating the analysis of both non-relativistic and relativistic magnetic properties.
main_problem: The difficulty in systematically tracking the symmetry evolution and physical properties of unconventional magnets when transitioning from the non-relativistic (exchange-driven) to the relativistic (spin-orbit coupling-driven) limit.
main_result: The introduction of FINDSPINGROUP, an online computational platform that uses the Oriented Spin Space Group (OSSG) framework to unify Spin Space Group (SSG) and Magnetic Space Group (MSG) descriptions and automate the identification of symmetry-constrained physical properties.
method: Development of a computational architecture implementing the OSSG framework, utilizing the .scif format for standardized data exchange, and integrating with tools like spglib, STensor, and VASP for symmetry analysis and DFT validation.
model_or_system: The framework is applied to unconventional magnetic crystals, including altermagnets (e.g., CrSb, MnTe, V2Se2O), spin-orbit magnets, and non-coplanar antiferromagnets (e.g., CoNb3S6).
key_observables: Momentum-dependent spin splitting, Anomalous Hall Effect (AHE), anomalous Nernst effect, magnetization (spin and orbital), electric polarization, and spin polarization.
important_parameters: Non-relativistic vs. relativistic limits, spin-lattice coupling strength, spin Wyckoff positions, and spin Brillouin zone symmetry.
main_assumptions: The framework assumes the validity of the Oriented Spin Space Group (OSSG) to remove orientational degeneracy between lattice and spin spaces.
figures_summary: Fig 1: Evolution of symmetry frameworks (SSG to OSSG to MSG). Fig 2: FINDSPINGROUP workflow. Fig 3: Hierarchy of magnetic phases. Fig 4: Symmetry analysis and spin splitting in V2Se2O. Fig 5: Analysis of MnSe2. Fig 6: Case study of CoNb3S6 showing AHE and spin splitting.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the scientific problem of symmetry tracking, presents the OSSG framework and the FINDSPINGROUP platform, details the technical implementation and data standardization (.scif), and demonstrates the utility of the tool through various case studies of complex magnetic materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
